title: Tenderly Toasted Thai Shrimp Tom Yum
description: A garlicky and pillowy southern Thai dish featuring brown sugar, cooked in a tandoor clay pot, perfect for a date night.
instructions: Preheat the tandoor clay pot to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, mix together the shrimp, brown sugar, fish sauce, lime juice, and garlic.
Add the lemon grass and tom yum paste to the bowl and stir to combine.
Pour in the coconut milk and stir until the shrimp are well coated.
Place the shrimp mixture in the preheated tandoor clay pot and cover with a lid.
Cook for 10 minutes, then remove the lid and stir in the chopped Thai basil.
Return the pot to the heat and cook for an additional 2 minutes, or until the shrimp are pink and cooked through.
Serve the Tenderly Toasted Thai Shrimp Tom Yum hot, garnished with additional Thai basil if desired.
